# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Jenkins Pipeline that runs directly on the master node for faster execution. The pipeline performs builds, runs tests, and deploys to production. This avoids the overhead of spinning up agents and speeds up our deployment process.
# Category: cicd_security

pipeline {
    agent any  // Change this line if you want a specific type or number of nodes in your pipeline (default is none, all available workers are used)   'master' node; otherwise it will be local workspace on the master machine. If specified here then only one job can run at once but not limited by total jobs that could simultaneously execute
    stages {  // This block contains multiple "stage" definitions in Jenkins pipeline and they each represent a part of work flow defined as set off from start to end point for CI/CD process like gitflow. These are executed sequentially, one after another which makes our workflow very linear (like waterfall).
        stage('Checkout') { // This block defines the code execution starting with 'stage' command followed by a string identifying it ie: "Initialize repository". It serves as human readable name for each Jenkins Job and this job starts from first step.    -> Calling external git commands to check out your project into workspace directory
        steps {  // Here we call the sh or bat script that has code related tasks, like cloning repo etc which will be executed by Groovy's 'steps'. Each task is defined as an action performed in a Jenkins environment. It defines what should happen for every single job and step execution within this pipeline
            withCredentials([usernamePassword(credentialsId: '', usernameVariable: '-P_USERNAME', passwordVariable: '-P_PASSWORD')]) { // Adds the code block that is necessary if there are any credentials required. This will be called as an agent which then uses these details to get authentication for cloning repo and building Jenkins
                sh('git clone https://github/com-userNameAndRepo/_repo_.git -b master')  // Here we call external git command that fetches your code from Github. It defines what is required by the process execution within this job step (the next block) or subsequent jobs(stages and tasks).
            }      
        }}  
     stage('Run Tests Using Maven + Junit') {  // This blocks define a Jenkins Job that will run tests, you could replace 'junit' with any other test framework. It is an example job to build your app using the provided mvn command and specify which junit xml file should be used for testing
        steps{      }  
    stage('Deploy To Production') {  // This block defines a Jenkins Job that will run tests, you could replace 'mvn' with any other tool to build your app. It is an example job just using mvn command and deploying the artifacts into remote server by copying it over SCP
        steps{      }   
   }}  // All stages are separated as shown above for clarity on pipeline flow, jobs will run sequentially within each stage block but can be executed at different points in Jenkins Pipeline. If a job fails or aborts the next step won't get execute because of previous failures/abortions (like if test cases failed).
}}  // closing bracket to define agent and pipeline stages for Groovy scripting language used by jenkinspipe which is written in groovevy. It defines a sequence control, ie Jenkins Workflows or Jobs within the Pipeline defined above will execute stepwise from start till end point
